metadata.name: Rjui
metadata.description: Platforms > Web deep-dive. ReactJsonUI + rjui CLI + Next.js App Router. Four sections: What you get / Next.js integration / Tailwind + generator output / Production caveats. ~10-min read. Self-referential: this documentation site is built with rjui.

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_swift -> /platforms/swift, next_kotlin -> /platforms/kotlin) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the platforms_rjui_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped URLs enumerated in transitions: /, /platforms/swift, /platforms/kotlin.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /platforms/swift and /platforms/kotlin. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

notes: Third and final Platforms entry. Flip PLATFORMS_ENTRIES row 3 (react) to 'live' — that completes the tab.

Meta-audience: web engineers who want to use rjui to dogfood JsonUI for their web-only product. The 'this site' angle makes the pitch tangible: 'if the output did not look and feel native you would not be reading this on it'.
